scenario: Draft a plaint for recovery of Rs.15,00,000 given as a hand loan. The plaintiff Ram Kumar, aged 45, resident of Jayanagar, Bengaluru, advanced Rs.15,00,000 to the defendant Suresh Patel, aged 38, resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru, on 15.03.2024 via NEFT bank transfer (UTR: AXIB20240315123456). There was no written agreement. The defendant acknowledged the loan via WhatsApp messages. Despite repeated oral demands and a legal notice dated 01.07.2024, the defendant has failed to repay. The plaintiff claims interest at 12% per annum from the date of default (20.06.2024). File in City Civil Court, Bengaluru.

scorecard.detail: At least one statutory section cited

draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE Civil Judge (Senior Division)
AT Bengaluru

O.S. No. __________ / 20____

Ram Kumar,
Aged about 45 years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Jayanagar, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

Suresh Patel,
Aged about 38 years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Koramangala, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF MONEY LENT / ADVANCE PAID WITH INTEREST AND COSTS

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

JURISDICTION

1. The Plaintiff is a person sui juris and competent to institute this suit.
2. The Defendant is a person sui juris and can be sued in this Court.
3. The Defendant resides at Koramangala, Bengaluru, and the cause of action arose within Bengaluru, invoking Section 20 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.
4. The suit is filed in the Court of the lowest grade competent to try it, and the suit value is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Court.
5. This Court has jurisdiction to try and dispose of the suit.

LIMITATION

6. The present suit is filed within the period of limitation prescribed under Article 11 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, as the suit is for recovery of money lent payable on demand.

FACTS OF THE TRANSACTION

7. On 15.03.2024, the Plaintiff advanced a sum of Rs. 15,00,000 to the Defendant as a hand loan.
8. The amount was transferred via NEFT from the Plaintiff's bank account to the Defendant's account, bearing UTR Number AXIB20240315123456, as evidenced by Annexure A.
9. The Defendant acknowledged the receipt of the said loan amount through WhatsApp messages, copies of which are annexed as Annexure B.
10. The loan was advanced on the understanding that the Defendant would repay the same on demand.

DEFAULT AND DEMAND

11. The Plaintiff made repeated oral demands for repayment of the loan amount from the Defendant.
12. Despite such demands, the Defendant failed to repay the loan, committing a default as on 20.06.2024.
13. Consequently, the Plaintiff issued a legal notice dated 01.07.2024 to the Defendant demanding repayment, as per Annexure C.
14. The Defendant has failed to comply with the legal notice and has not repaid the outstanding dues.

QUANTIFICATION OF CLAIM

15. The principal amount outstanding due from the Defendant is Rs. 15,00,000.
16. The Plaintiff is entitled to interest at the rate of 12% per annum from the date of default i.e., 20.06.2024 till the date of realisation.
17. The interest claimed is simple interest and not compound interest, as per the understanding between the parties.
18. The total claim comprises the principal amount of Rs. 15,00,000 along with accrued interest calculated at 12% per annum.

LEGAL BASIS

19. The Plaintiff is entitled to the relief sought under Section 37 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872, as the Defendant is bound to perform the promise to repay the loan.
20. The advance of money is supported by consideration, evidenced by the bank transaction record with UTR Number AXIB20240315123456.
21. The rate of interest claimed at 12% per annum is within permissible limits for unsecured lending and is not usurious.
22. The Defendant's acknowledgment via WhatsApp messages precludes any defence of non-existence of the debt.

CAUSE OF ACTION

23. The cause of action first arose on 20.06.2024 when the Defendant failed to repay the loan despite the understanding to repay on demand.
24. The cause of action subsists and continues as the Defendant has failed to comply with the legal notice and has not repaid the outstanding dues.
25. The suit is filed within the period of limitation prescribed under the Limitation Act, 1963.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

26. The suit is valued at {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}} for the purpose of jurisdiction and court fee.
27. The requisite court fee of {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} has been paid on the plaint.
28. The plaint is properly stamped and tendered with the requisite court fee under {{COURT_FEE_ACT_NAME}}.

INTEREST

29. The Plaintiff claims interest on the suit amount as follows:
    (a) Pendente lite interest under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 from the date of institution of the suit until the date of decree;
    (b) Future interest under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 from the date of decree until realization of the entire decretal amount.

PRAYER

30. In the premises aforesaid, the Plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Hon'ble Court be pleased to:
31. (a) Pass a decree for recovery of money in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant for a sum of Rs. 15,00,000 (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs Only);
32. (b) Award interest on the said sum at the rate of 12% per annum from 20.06.2024 till the date of realisation, and award future interest under Section 34 CPC from the date of decree till the date of realisation;
33. (c) Award costs of the suit to the Plaintiff;
34. (d) Grant such other and furthe
